verses.text: God bids Jeremiah make yokes and send word to the surrounding kings by their envoys. A living sign. God dramatizes His message that all must submit to Babylon.
God bids Jeremiah make yokes and send word to the surrounding kings by their envoys. A living sign. God dramatizes His message that all must submit to Babylon.
God bids Jeremiah make yokes and send word to the surrounding kings by their envoys. A living sign. God dramatizes His message that all must submit to Babylon.
God bids Jeremiah make yokes and send word to the surrounding kings by their envoys. A living sign. God dramatizes His message that all must submit to Babylon.

'I have made the earth... and given it unto whom it seemed meet'; now He has given all these lands to Nebuchadnezzar for a season. God disposes of kingdoms. Even a pagan empire rules only by His grant.

'I have made the earth... and given it unto whom it seemed meet'; now He has given all these lands to Nebuchadnezzar for a season. God disposes of kingdoms. Even a pagan empire rules only by His grant.

'I have made the earth... and given it unto whom it seemed meet'; now He has given all these lands to Nebuchadnezzar for a season. God disposes of kingdoms. Even a pagan empire rules only by His grant.
The nation that will not serve Babylon He will punish, but the one that bows its neck He will let remain. Submission is wisdom. To resist the rod God has appointed only deepens the wound.

He urges Zedekiah, 'bring your necks under the yoke... and live,' and not to heed prophets who promise otherwise. Their counsel is deadly. False assurances against God's plain word lead to ruin.

Let none believe the prophets who say the temple vessels will soon return; 'they prophesy a lie.' Their hopes are false. Wishful prophecy cannot overturn the judgment God has decreed.
Let none believe the prophets who say the temple vessels will soon return; 'they prophesy a lie.' Their hopes are false. Wishful prophecy cannot overturn the judgment God has decreed.
Let none believe the prophets who say the temple vessels will soon return; 'they prophesy a lie.' Their hopes are false. Wishful prophecy cannot overturn the judgment God has decreed.
The remaining vessels shall also go to Babylon and stay 'until the day that I visit them.' Loss precedes restoration. God will bring them back in His own time, not by men's flattering schemes.
